title: GIS Explorer
description: The GIS Explorer enables spatial analysis of clinical and population health data by mapping patient cohorts and health outcomes to geographic regions. Researchers can identify geographic variation in disease burden, access to care, and health disparities by overlaying clinical data with geospatial reference layers.
tips: Aggregate patient data to appropriate geographic units such as postal codes or health service areas to protect patient privacy while enabling spatial analysis.
Use choropleth maps to visualise the geographic distribution of cohort prevalence and identify high-burden regions.
Overlay social determinants of health layers to contextualise observed geographic variation in clinical outcomes.
Apply spatial filters to restrict cohort generation to specific geographic catchment areas relevant to your research question.
Export GIS outputs as standard geospatial file formats for integration with external mapping and planning tools.
